Now we're going to go down and we're going to skip a line because this is a letter, not an ad. We're going to indent five paragraphs, spaces, whatever. Same thing. Okay, now, before we take just a really brief break, someone started to make a little bit of a joke about farm animals. You know, we stopped that. And I'm going to tell you a true story involving a farm animal. And it relates to what we're doing here, and I'm going to leave you to ponder how it relates and ask you after we take a five minute break. See if any of you can figure out how it relates. But the following story is true. In Akron, Ohio, many years ago, I took a class, and I can't remember who it was from, Toastmasters or somebody on public speaking. I was much younger then, and you know, I worked for a company, and I just thought I ought to learn how to talk in public. And they would have all of us go give short talks on different subjects. They'd pick a subject, you know, and then we'd give the talk. And one of the subjects was my most embarrassing moment. And I gave a talk on my most embarrassing moment, and the leader of the class said when I was finished, that that was the second most embarrassing thing he'd ever had talked about in his class. Okay, I'm going to tell you what only came in second. I was a military policeman in Europe. What? To what? To the state. Oh, I was a military policeman in Europe, and when I was in Germany, I was on duty one time with my patrol partner, and they asked me and him to come into the MP station to take off our uniforms and stand in the lineup in sort of like civilian clothes. Now that... Wait a minute, I have to back up, have to back up a little bit. Maybe you should start with something new tape, I think that's what the message is. Is that what you're trying to say? Started on the new tape? Yeah, we're getting ready for another tape. Can you just put another one in? Yeah, do you want to quit for a minute? Then I'll tell the story and then we'll come back, okay? That's one of the reasons I was reluctant to tape this because I don't like, you know, I don't like interrupting what I'm doing to accommodate the technical part of it, but I think it is important that we have a video and audio record and like I said, everybody's paid to come here, I'm giving you a free copy of it so you'll have it, so just don't even get your bitching geared up, okay? Are you guys going down Ocean Drive tonight? South Beach? Huh? Yeah. Yeah, I think Ocean Drive is the neatest drive. I think it's personally my favorite street in the world. Go to the paparazzi and listen to Alex Fox play tonight. That's one place you should go. Oh yeah, go to mangoes. Okay, it's a little different where you should go depending on whether you're single or married. Yes, if you're single you want to go to Madonna. Do you know that Madonna's is a topless bar and that Madonna tried to sue them for using the name Madonna? She'd have to sue the Catholic Church, you know what she'd have to? She lost the suit. Anyway, back to my, and I'll make this story really brief. When I was a military policeman in Europe, the first thing we did when we went on duty is we stood in line up and they inspected us and then they would read down, you know, the list of crimes had been committed and people were supposed to be on the lookout for etc. etc. And there was one crime that was committed that they told us about it and we just had to start laughing and what happened is that somebody broke into a farm and he went into a barn and there was a cow in the barn and he began having relations with the cow. This is true, he really did this. The farmer came in and caught him. He knocked, he hit the farmer in the mouth, knocked him down, dropped over a lantern that had a flame inside of it and set the place on fire and ran out. Well, one day I was on duty in the Jeep with my patrol partner and they asked us to come in and take off our uniform jacket and stand in a lineup, which is not unusual. They do that with police a lot, you know, you stand in a lineup so someone, you know, gets a fair identification through the one-way glass. And what was unusual about this lineup is that when they were finished, they said everyone could leave except Private Halbert. I had to stay and I immediately began to worry because I was supplementing my income by black marketing cigarettes, you know, I'd take the stamps that I got to cigarettes and I'd sell them to the Germans and I thought, oh, they've got me. So the guy sits me down and I said, what's this all about? And he says, well, I want to read your rights to you first. I think, whoa, this is kind of heavy duty. He reads the rights to me and I said, well, what am I charged with? And he said, you're charged with breaking and entering, trespass, arson, aggravated assault, and sodomy. And I said, sodomy? I said, wait a minute, are you talking about that cow deal? He was. I said, you're kidding. He was not kidding. What had happened is the German farmer made a positive identification of me, he said, and as it turns out, well, I'll get to that part later, but well, no, as it turns out, what had actually happened is that I, when you're in the military, you write your serial number and all your clothing and one of my overseas cap was too small for me and I'd thrown it away and the guy who actually did this was wearing my cap and it fell off when he knocked the lamp over and they traced it to me from my serial number. Plus, they probably tipped off the German because, you know, for whatever. The bottom line is that I was placed under arrest for screwing a cow. Honestly, I was. I was. This is really true. And they took me back to the barracks and I was a great practical joker over there and a lot of guys had scores to settle with me and me and men, they were doing it and one of the things that they did is they kept me confined to my quarters and I wasn't allowed to do anything except go to the mess hall and eat and stand roll call and in roll call, I would stand there and there's I was in a 600 man battalion with three companies. I was in company C and the roll call would go like Jones, here's sergeant Smith, here's sergeant McGillicuddy, here's sergeant Halbert and 600 guys would go like that, you know. There was no way to deal with that except to try and develop a sense of humor about it. You know, guys would say, well, how was she? And I said, well, you know, you had to get off the bucket and walk clear around the front when you wanted to kiss her and stuff like that. And it turns out the guy who did it was a Puerto Rican private where it's lucky they caught him and I didn't because I think I would have killed him. But anyhow, that was the second most embarrassing thing this guy had ever heard. Would you like to hear the first one? Imagine, I mean, I couldn't believe what happened to somebody that's more embarrassing. Oh, it was even worse because I was engaged then to the Nazi bitch who later became my first wife, you know. And her family was like kind of a high society family. You should have seen me trying to explain to them why they had to come down to the MP station and testify to my whereabouts because I'm under arrest for sodomizing a farm animal. You know, that really did not play very well. They had hooked me up to a lie detector machine. Also, when they do that, the guy spends 20 minutes telling you how infallible it is. He's got this J Edgar Hoover voice, you can't beat this machine, etc. And you know, and I said, Hey, man, you know, like I'm a little bit nervous. I said, you know, what about me just being jumpy? And he said, Oh, we asked test questions for you know, so that you to establish a pattern. So you don't have to worry about that. And I said, Oh, okay, fine, you know. And so I'm all there hooked up with these electrodes to my chest and my arm and everything and this thing around for your respiration. Here's the here's like the way he questioned me. Is your name Gary? Yes. Are you in the Navy? No. Are you a military policeman? Yes. Were you in a barn with a cow on the night of the 17th? You know, anyway, let me tell you what the most embarrassing I would I said, I thought someone had something more embarrassing happened to them. And it was a woman. This is another true story. And she was from Connecticut or someplace. And she lived in a pretty high society community. And she's one of these people that would commute to Manhattan and back. And she's going home in the car, the commuter car is just jam packed, you know, with people, you know, the and the sort of the upper echelon of society, at least there. And the plane is so the computer, the car is so crowded, she's in the back, she doesn't have a place to sit down. And someone for some reason pulled the emergency stop cord. And so the plane, the train just stops abruptly. And there's this big crush of people coming back against her. She's clear in the back, the men's rooms back here. There's a guy in there sitting on the pot with his pants down around his ankles. She ends up sitting on his lap and cannot get off for about 10 minutes because of the crush of people there. And so the guy asked her, what did they talk about? You know what she said? She said, he didn't say a word. I didn't say a word. I mean, and I thought about it, that would be more embarrassing. And what would you say? I mean, I personally couldn't deal with either side of that. There's a there is a reason that relates to writing this copy that I told you those stories. I want to see we're going to take like five minutes just as a break. And I want to see if anyone can figure that out and then we'll go on. How long can we go tonight? I'll tell you why I told that story. In selling, humor almost never works. It unless you're just trying an experiment that you can afford, you shouldn't use it. Humor, however, dramatically improves the learning process. And every once in a while, if you can loosen people up with a little bit of humor, you will in most learning environments are so serious, you know, and they're oppressive. The number one block to creativity is fear. Fear of being criticized. You notice I don't really criticize people. I might make good natured fun of them. But nobody, I mean, says anything that I really criticize as being wrong, because we're not going to make any mistakes here. The only real mistake is not getting this job done. And I want to tell you something. What we're doing right now is exactly the way it gets done. I was leery about giving this seminar for quite some time, because some of so many of my other seminars have like more pyrotechnics, you know, they had guys screaming and yelling and demonstrations and all the rest of it. And in a way, this is more boring than that. But in truth, this is where everybody messes up. You know, this is what, this is literally really how it gets done. And I try and make it as interesting and as palatable as I possibly can. But the fact of the matter is, a lot of writing copy is just what we're doing. It's just this get down in the dirt, roll your sleeves up, grunt, work, dog it out. And this is what this is what it is, even with a even with a good template, like from someone like John Carlton, and with me leading it, you know, that can you imagine how difficult that is, somebody starting from scratch is the I want to give you guys a couple of things to think about. Well, actually, before I do that, would you just start talking about the putter, the guy who invented it, what's different about it, and so forth. Yeah. The reason I did that, without getting much information from him is that this is the 11,000 time I've done this. And I know, I know where I'm going, except for the specifics. And I know that what I have written so far may have to be altered. But I've created a slightly altered template from John Carlton's and I know that what I've done here is really boiler proof boilerplate almost foolproof introduction to copy. Now we're going to get some specifics. So tell us just babble on about this thing and throw in questions at any time. Most people, first of all, there's more putts taken than any other stroke on a golf game. Most putters 99% of the putters on the market cause backspin when the ball is struck. The backspin has to turn into overspin at some point, which it does as the ball is backspinning and rolling on the ground and eventually the friction turns it so it's moving forward. That also causes the ball to get side spin. In addition to the backspin, until it finally gets into some sort of overspin and causes it to be inaccurate. Almost all putters have a positive lock. So when the ball is struck, it leaves the turf. Because most putts, if you didn't have it have a positive lock, it would get hung up in the green and get also get side spin and some other inaccurate spin. And there's a few negative lofted putters that do give overspin when the ball is hit, but the ball gets hit down into the turf, which causes it to bounce out of the turf and also then has the same exact inaccurate and distance problems as a backspinning putter. So this guy in tinkering in his workshop, which I'm sure is what it does make him keep in the basement, wanted to figure out why you couldn't have a putter that would be a positive lock and get off the surface of the green, but be in complete overspin. And initially it was pulled by lots of engineers and golf club people that you couldn't do that. You couldn't have the ball go up and have overspin at the same time. And eventually, after a million prototypes and tries, he finally got one that does it. So when the ball is struck, it leaves the green ever so slightly. It's in complete overspin. Has no side spin. And he did machine calibrated tests, so they would take the human element out of the testing. And it was anywhere from two to 10 times more accurate on different distance putts. For example, on 24 foot putts on a real green using the 10 of the best selling putters in the world and in this on the same stroke, two out of three of these balls went in the hole and the farthest away from the hole that landed was six inches. And the closest of the 10 best selling putters was 18 to 28 inches away. None of them went in the hole. So he then went and got a patent on this and couldn't sell it to anybody. And he got that desperate, came to me and I acquired the patent from him. And we just hit right on our first test ad. Two weeks ago, my strategy was to test lead generation ads with a one form sales letter. And the price of the there's two models, one's a steel shafted model, which is 377. And the graphite shafted with the oversized grip model is 457. And he has no appreciable testimonials, other than a few that some pros and a couple of people have redone his tests and verified the tests. And I made my first ad, we've got 210 leads on a $25,000 ad and we got 5% conversion so far on the first letter. And I haven't said the second letters are just going out this weekend. And that's the whole story. What was the most interesting part of everything that he said to various of you? The balls went in the hole. The ball went in the hole goes in the hole. 210 leads, which ever would be very high 5% purchase. What's the most interesting thing he said to anybody else? Better than the other professional matters. Okay, they're actually except for taking notes that I can do in a coma. I mean, I only listen to one thing he said. Guess what that was? The arithmetic. I did the numbers. The price came out not by magic because I did these numbers and I figured out what I had to charge to make this all work. Because I was figuring I'd get between a 7 to 10 percent conversion rate over three mailings and I'm also I have no testimonials yet. We have no video but you guys go up high. We know you better play over. He does have life too. It was the first step. That was the only thing I was concerned with initially was whether I had life or not. Okay. That's it and this man by the way is an experienced mail order professional and that's the only question you need to be answering at this point is does this thing have a prayer or not? See you can you can nitpick and refine etc etc but nine times out of ten the answer to most marketing endeavors is we have no interest whatsoever in what you're selling. That's what the market will say to you. Okay. So now he knows that it's got life and he's done he's ready to refine it and he's coming here to find out. Now what I want to do now is I want to I'm going to take a little side trips on here instead of you know just pulling it through. When someone in Hollywood wants to make a movie they start putting together the elements of that movie and one of the thing one of the elements is a script. Okay. And they they need a writer and let's suppose they go out and they get a good script writer like William Goldman and then another element is they need a director and let's suppose that they go out and they get Steven Spielberg. Another element is a male lead. Let's say they get Paul Newman. Another element is a female lead. Let's say that they get Demi Moore and basically what they are assembling is a package. Now with a package like I just described that's a dream package that gives you a very high percentage. I should say a higher percentage of success than someone going around saying you know get this idea that people would like to see a movie about leaves as they leave the trees in autumn and trickle down and fall upon an old man's chest and he begins to reflect on his life and the Cocker Spaniel that was hit by a car. And people think like that and they go ahead and make movies you know. People think like that and they go ahead and create marketing promotions. What you will do if you are interested in marketing promotions and really making this is you will become an opportunity detective. You will begin to see hey wait there's an element I can use and you may not use no for what. For example a man who's one of my heroes sought me out recently his name is Captain Tony. He's the mayor for life of Key West. His bar is where was featured in Hemingway book. He hung out in the bar when Tony owned it. He's been a friend of Truman Capote. He's Tennessee Williams. He was a guy smuggler to Q. He's a famous smuggler. I love it when our politicians are openly corrupt. Tony's youngest son is 10. His oldest is 60. He'll be 80 in August and has a 27 year old girlfriend. My kind of guy. He had some problems recently. One of his kids got involved in the drug deal and they hung the guy from the ceiling and he had to go get the money to bail him out you know etc etc. And his whole life is an adventure. They've made one movie about it. They're getting ready to make another. He's writing the book and I got a real big kick because I went down there with one of our models and when he saw me I walked in this place at Peer House. He said you look like a god coming in there. I wanted to talk to you and he wanted me to be the last chapter in his book you know because he's finishing up the book. But you know the way I thought is Tony he needed a favor and I did it. He said you know use me and I thought wow I'm selling my newsletter. How about if it comes from the mayor for life of Key West. Do you understand what I'm saying. I saw that as an opportunity. Put the elements together. Now one of the I'm going to some of these discourses are actually much more valuable than you believe that you think the things are valuable like which words do you italicize and stuff. And I told you that what I want to do as much as possible is nothing but teach from here on out. And I want to become the best teacher that I can and that means that I need to devote more of my time and energy to it. It also means that I need to not get sidetracked on various projects because I get involved in projects other than my main interest which is self aggrandizement. And be honest you know. And then what happens is I don't put them together. And through the course of my year do you know what my M.O. is on marketing projects. I get an idea. I create the product. I create a promotion. I test it. I get good numbers. I abandon the project. That's my M.O. And I have over the years. I honestly got someone just get rich following me around with with a legal pad taking notes you know. And so some of these projects I've started and they have I know that I'm not going to finish them. I know it. And I'm going to tell you about one of them and maybe tell you about a couple of them. And one of them doesn't involve. I mean I'm not trying to sell them. I just want somebody to do them. You know sometimes I try and give away ideas. I'm going to try and see if anyone's interested in this. If you will look through this this package of sales letters that you either have or have or will be given. Don't look through it now. Just put your hands back on. Don't look through it now. You will find a whole bunch of campaigns that John Carlton and I have worked on that teaches people how to fight. We call it the how to kill stuff. And the way that got started is Jay Abraham wrote an ad for these bodybuilders in Visalia, Ohio about the Bulgarian power lift method. He somehow lost interest in it. I inherited the ad. I began running it selling this report. The guys made a lot of money. Then they went on to selling vitamin supplements and then they discovered that the market they were selling to has an insatiable appetite for heart for learning how to hurt other people and actually protect themselves. You know. So what they would do is they'll find a guy who was trained the Navy SEALs and they make a video of him. And then John writes a letter and says you know and he and I have a lot of fun you know making these up. Okay so we'll talk. We'll devise the most embarrassing humiliating scenario a man can go what would you do if this happened to you. You're walking with your woman and your two children through a city street you're coming from an airport and they're in front of you. So we've already got the bad guy. And believe me we've got stories about him and we've already written a lot of the stories. They are true that are just absolutely unbelievable. His training and how he got to be this way is unreal. One brief story. It was in the newspaper so I can tell this one. He's in Michigan and he goes into a bar where he goes into all the time. And they always have a guy outside a bouncer checking IDs. Now Lee being 60 now and a little bit younger then didn't show anybody his ID. You know he's obviously over 21. And so he'd always just walk right past and drink. Well he one day he did this and unbeknownst to him there was a cover charge that night. There was a band or something. And the bouncer got a little bit irate at this and Lee is sitting at the bar drinking a long neck beer and the bouncer comes up behind him and grabs him like that. You know it hurt him. Which was very bad thing to do with Lee Crawl. And so a split second later the guy has eaten the beer bottle and he's unconscious on the floor. Well this is a Pollock bar. And one of the Polish guys says to Lee you just made a serious mistake man. We're going to tear your ass apart. And the barmaid knew that these were bad guys. And so she called the police immediately because she didn't want Lee to get hurt in her bar. In the meantime Lee said well if you guys want a piece of me let's not tear the bar up let's go outside. So he walked outside the bar. And these guys started coming out after him. And the police did arrive. And when they got there there were 39 Pollocks laying in the parking lot and Lee was standing there drinking a beer. He wouldn't teach. He will not teach what he knows to very many people because he thinks it's too dangerous for them to know. It doesn't involve months of training. It involves two or three days. It doesn't involve you know like bowing and curtsying and learning to eat sushi or none of that stuff. And he is truly the nicest human being you'd ever meet. He lives up northern Michigan where it's cold in a remote area. Lays on the ground. He's got this big belly. He sprinkles breadcrumbs around it. Let's the ducks come and eat the breadcrumbs in the winter time. His wife Sandy is such a good cook that the real danger in going up there is you'll get fat. Now one of the things when you have something to sell, like the same thing that John and I sell on these bad asses, we have the same thing to sell every month. A hardware store basically has the same thing to sell as the other hardware store. Orthodontist A has the same thing to sell as orthodontist B. There's two big things that can make you stand out. Well, I live in the modeling capital of the world. A little... I'll give you a little geography lesson. Miami Metro is 2,000 square miles. Miami Beach is 54 square miles. South Beach where I live is one and a half square miles. And according to a quote by Playboy magazine, within that one and a half square miles, there are more beautiful women per capita than any other geographic location on earth in history. Now the thing that about that that got me thinking is, wouldn't... How do you like this? You know, the world's deadliest man finally agrees to reveal his secrets. And the reason for that is, is that he's... You know, he's... He's like... Is the plight of the models. Models are among the most beautiful and desirable women in the world, but they also are in a very high risk operation. They often find themselves thousands of miles from home, working with strangers in various states of undress, including total nudity, with blah, blah, blah, and they're very vulnerable.
